rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding missing annex blocks or fixing syntax errors without changing unrelated architecture
generic_rules: Use only valid AGREE syntax and visible identifiers in repairs
Place assumptions and guarantees in the correct annex scope based on the component type or implementation context
Ensure all guarantees have valid expressions and types
Use named constants and equations for complex expressions to improve readability and maintainability
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` for valid AGREE syntax
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths or implementation-level references in component type contracts
Do not add guarantees with unresolved identifiers or invalid types
Do not delete unrelated architecture when repairing validator diagnostics
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports
Subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
Case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names
